moments.description: Amaya walked to school, mentioning the honor system in Korea where people don't steal. She was impressed by this aspect of Korean culture.

replay.tip: Korea University is a great place to experience the honor system firsthand. Be sure to respect the rules and leave your belongings unattended to see how it works.

moments.title: Classes at Korea University
moments.description: Amaya attended classes at Korea University, mentioning that she was confused but eventually found a friend to eat with. She was grateful for the opportunity to learn and experience Korean culture.

replay.tip: Be prepared for a challenging academic environment and don't be afraid to ask for help. You can also try to make friends with your classmates to enhance your learning experience.

visits.spot: Exploring Taebaek, Korea

moments.title: Exploring Taebaek Streets
moments.description: We walked around and saw what there is to see and do in Taebaek, and we were quite surprised at how fun it was to explore. We visited a Korean Western Franchise called Han's Deli.

moments.title: Trying Koreanized Western Food
moments.description: We tried some very Koreanized Western food at Han's Deli, including kadan or potato pancakes and makuku, which literally translates to just putting whatever you have together with the noodles. Both were nice and light and quite good for the cheap price.

replay.tip: Try the kadan and makuku dishes for a unique Korean twist on Western food.

moments.title: Walking on the Mountain Trails
moments.description: We walked on the mountain trails at night, which was perfectly safe for residents to do at all times of the day. The trails were well-lit and offered great views of the surrounding area.

moments.title: Visiting the Art and Cultural Center
moments.description: We visited the Art and Cultural Center in Taebaek, which had a full set of trails going up and down the mountain. We parked at the center and decided to check out the trails.

visits.spot: Korea's Most Underrated Ski Resort!

moments.title: Skiing at O2 Resort
moments.description: The creators visited O2 Resort for the first time and were blown away by the empty slopes. They rode the available slopes and gave a fair review of what they think about it.

replay.cost: ₩15,000 per person (train from Cheongnyangni to Tek station)
replay.tip: Take the train from Cheongnyangni to Tek station and then a 10-minute taxi ride to O2 Resort.

moments.title: Comparing to High1 Resort
moments.description: The creators compared O2 Resort to High1 Resort, which is just 20 minutes away. They discussed the pros and cons of each resort.

replay.cost: ₩10,100 for a 10-minute taxi ride from Tek to O2 Resort
replay.tip: Use the Cacao Tea app or Kride app to book a taxi from Tek to O2 Resort.

visits.spot: Top 5 Day Trips from Seoul

moments.title: Hwaseong Fortress
moments.description: The creator visited Hwaseong Fortress in Suwon, a 5.7 kilometer long fortress wall with four gates built in the late 1700s by King Chongjo of the Joseon dynasty to honor the remains of his father, Prince Sado. The fortress is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and a popular tourist destination.

replay.tip: Take a guided tour to learn more about the history and significance of the fortress.

moments.title: DMZ Day Tours
moments.description: The creator took a DMZ Day Tour, which includes visits to the Demilitarized Zone, the Joint Security Area, and the Third Tunnel. The tour provides a unique perspective on the history and politics of the Korean Peninsula.

replay.tip: Book a reputable tour operator to ensure a safe and informative experience.

moments.title: Nami Island
moments.description: The creator visited Nami Island, a peaceful and scenic island in the middle of the Han River. The island is famous for its beautiful gardens, walking trails, and stunning views of the surrounding landscape.

replay.tip: Rent a bike to explore the island and enjoy the scenery.

visits.spot: The Ultimate Guide to Korea’s High-Speed Trains

moments.description: The creator showed a step-by-step tutorial on getting off the subway at Seoul Station and transferring to the KTX.

moments.title: Riding the KTX
moments.description: The creator explained the differences between the high-speed trains and showed how to purchase tickets and ride the KTX.

visits.spot: Don’t Go to Korea Without Knowing These 15 Tips!

visits.spot: 10 More Great Places in Seoul

moments.title: Exploring Seochon Village
moments.description: Seochon is a cozy little village known as Seochon, a quieter place to walk. It's one of the oldest neighborhoods in Seoul, being the birthplace of King Sejong.

replay.tip: Take the subway line number three to Gyeongbokgung Station and walk out exit one, two, or three.

moments.title: Discovering Majang Meat Market
moments.description: Majang Meat Market is a bustling market filled with various meats and seafood. It's a great place to try some local delicacies.

replay.tip: Be prepared to try some new and exotic foods.

moments.title: Visiting Seodaemun Independence Park
moments.description: Seodaemun Independence Park is a historic site that commemorates the Korean independence movement. It's a great place to learn about Korea's history.

replay.tip: Take a moment to reflect on Korea's history and independence movement.

moments.title: Trying Street Food in Sindang-dong
moments.description: Sindang-dong is a popular street food destination with a variety of local delicacies. It's a great place to try some street food and snacks.

replay.tip: Be prepared to try some spicy and savory street food.

moments.title: Visiting Nodeul Island
moments.description: Nodeul Island is a peaceful and serene island located in the middle of the Han River. It's a great place to relax and enjoy nature.

replay.tip: Take a moment to appreciate the natural beauty of the island.

visits.spot: This Wealthy Seoul Neighborhood Was Once Incredibly Dangerous

moments.title: Exploring Bangbae-dong
moments.description: The creator walked through Bangbae-dong, a wealthy neighborhood in Seocho-gu, Seoul, and discovered its hidden stories and folk tales.

moments.title: Visiting the South Gate of Seoul
moments.description: The creator visited the South Gate of Seoul, also known as Namtaeryeong, a historic pass that connects Seoul to the rest of the country.

moments.title: Discovering Shamanic Sites
moments.description: The creator discovered shamanic sites and folk tales in Bangbae-dong, including the story of the 'Fox Pass' and the 'Three Ghosts'.

moments.title: Hiking to the Observatory
moments.description: The creator hiked to the observatory, where they saw a beautiful view of the surrounding mountains and valleys.

moments.title: Visiting a Coffee Shop
moments.description: The creator visited a coffee shop in Bangbae-dong, where they enjoyed a cup of coffee and took in the atmosphere.

visits.spot: Walking the Seoul City Wall at Night

moments.title: Walking the Entire Seoul City Wall
moments.description: The creator walked the 18.6 km historic Seoul City Wall at night, following the ridges of four mountains and connecting eight gates around the old capital. They started at Hyeongungmun and walked in a clockwise direction.

replay.tip: The official trail is divided into six sections, and you can walk them all in one go or do them one by one.

moments.title: Exploring the Seoul City Wall
moments.description: The creator explored the Seoul City Wall, admiring the historic fortress and its various sections. They noticed different types of stones and imagined the wall's history.

replay.tip: You can easily find the Seoul City Wall trail map online.

moments.title: Discovering the History of the Seoul City Wall
moments.description: The creator learned about the history of the Seoul City Wall, which was built in 1396 during the Joseon Dynasty. They discovered that the wall was built by tens of thousands of workers in just 98 days.

replay.tip: You can visit the Seoul City Wall at any time, but it's recommended to go during the day for better visibility.

moments.title: Enjoying the Night View of Seoul
moments.description: The creator enjoyed the night view of Seoul from the top of Naksan, which offered a stunning panorama of the city. They appreciated the peaceful atmosphere and the clear sky.

replay.tip: The best time to visit Naksan for the night view is during the winter months when the sky is clear.

moments.title: Trying Street Food in Changsin-dong
moments.description: The creator visited Changsin-dong, a neighborhood known for its street food and industrial heritage. They tried some local food and drinks, such as kimchi and makgeolli.

replay.tip: Changsin-dong is a great place to try street food and experience the local culture.

moments.title: Shamanism Museum

moments.title: Buddhist Temples

moments.title: Nixon-era Story
moments.description: The creator discovered a strange Nixon-era story while exploring the neighborhood and learned about its significance in Korean history. They were fascinated by the unique and intriguing story.

visits.spot: I Entered Seoul's Forbidden American Town

moments.title: Exploring Yongsan Garrison
moments.description: The creator explored Yongsan Garrison, a former U.S. Army base in Yongsan District, right in the heart of Seoul. They reviewed the parts recently made available to the public.

moments.title: Discovering the History of Yongsan Garrison
moments.description: The creator learned about the history of Yongsan Garrison, including its past as a sealed-off world and a black hole in the middle of the city. They also discovered the plans to transform the area into a public park.

moments.title: Walking through the National Museum of Korea
moments.description: The creator walked through the National Museum of Korea, which is located within the Yongsan Garrison complex. They admired the museum's architecture and the surrounding park.

moments.title: Visiting the Dragon Hill Lodge
moments.description: The creator visited the Dragon Hill Lodge, a popular spot for tourists. They enjoyed the natural surroundings and the peaceful atmosphere.

moments.title: Learning about the History of the Black Hawk Village
moments.description: The creator learned about the history of the Black Hawk Village, a former military housing complex. They discovered the plans to transform the area into a public park.

visits.spot: Stop Going to the Same Markets in Seoul

moments.title: Mangang-dong Slaughter Market
moments.description: The creator visited the Mangang-dong Slaughter Market, which is known for its unique character, history, and stories. The market has a wide variety of meats on display, including internal organs.

replay.tip: Take the subway to Yongdu Station and exit 4 to find the market.

moments.title: Yakrin Market
moments.description: The creator visited the Yakrin Market, which is the largest herbal medicine market in Korea. The market has over 800 herbal medicine shops and offers a wide variety of traditional Korean remedies.

replay.tip: Take the subway to Jegi-dong Station to find the market.

visits.spot: Hidden Gem in Seoul

moments.title: Oldest Western-style Church in Korea

moments.title: Seosomun History Park
moments.description: The creator visited Seosomun History Park, a former execution ground during the Joseon Dynasty. The park is a somber reminder of Korea's history and a great place to learn about the country's past.

moments.title: Visiting the Jinheung Sunwon
moments.description: The creator visited the Jinheung Sunwon, a large traditional Korean house that served as a place for performing rituals. The house is now a museum and a great example of traditional Korean architecture.

moments.title: Learning about the Tragic History of the Yun Family

moments.title: Exploring K-Arts Campus
moments.description: The creator and their friend Anna, a K-Arts alumna, explore the K-Arts campus, which has a unique history and was once part of the Korean Central Intelligence Agency (KCIA). They visit the damaged royal tomb grounds and learn about the darker side of this fascinating place.

visits.spot: How Japan Stole Seoul's Most Beloved Mountain

moments.title: Exploring Namsan's History

They also visited Seoul's last remaining citizen apartments, hidden statues with forgotten stories, a former KCIA torture center, abandoned shelters, old city wall remnants, and more.

moments.title: Discovering Hidden Statues
moments.description: The creator discovered hidden statues with forgotten stories on Namsan. They also found a statue of An Jung-geun, a Korean independence activist who assassinated Ito Hirobumi in 1909.

moments.title: Visiting the Sakura Tree

replay.tip: Try yachang at street food stalls outside schools in Korea for a unique winter snack experience.

moments.title: Korean Hotteok
moments.description: The creator and British high schoolers also tried Korean hotteok, a sweet pancake-like snack. They enjoyed the sweet and crispy texture.

place.name: Street food stalls outside schools in Korea

replay.tip: Try Korean hotteok at street food stalls outside schools in Korea for a sweet and crispy snack.

visits.spot: British Footballers Stunned by REAL Korean BBQ!

moments.title: Trying Tteokbokki
moments.description: The creators tried tteokbokki, a popular Korean street food, and found it to be very spicy. They were surprised by the various levels of spiciness available in Korea.

replay.tip: Try the spicier version of tteokbokki for a more authentic experience.

moments.title: Korean Meal Experience
moments.description: The creators were introduced to a full Korean meal, which included various dishes such as starters and main courses. They were impressed by the quality and variety of the food.

replay.tip: Be sure to try a variety of Korean dishes to experience the full range of flavors.

visits.spot: Korean Viral Desserts

moments.title: Injeolmi Toast
moments.description: The creator tried Injeolmi Toast, a classic traditional Korean dessert made on the side of the road by grandmas or grandpas. They found it to be a bit too sweet.

moments.title: Dubai Chewy Cookie
moments.description: The creator tried Dubai Chewy Cookie, a viral dessert that has taken Korea by storm. They found it to be very nice and crunchy.

visits.spot: INSIDE South Korea's #1 BEST All You Can Eat LOBSTER & KING CRAB Hotel Buffet

moments.title: La Seine Buffet
moments.description: The creator visited La Seine buffet, which is the most expensive hotel buffet in South Korea. He tried various dishes including lobster, king crab, and sushi.

replay.tip: Book a table in advance to avoid long waiting times.

moments.title: McDonald's
moments.description: The creator stopped by McDonald's for a light breakfast and tried their sweet potato fries and basil cream cheese egg mcmuffin.

replay.tip: Try the sweet potato fries with pancake syrup for a unique experience.

visits.spot: INSIDE the MOST EXPENSIVE All You Can Eat KOREAN BREAKFAST BUFFET

moments.title: Zest Breakfast Buffet
moments.description: The creator visited Zest to compare the food, quality, atmosphere, and overall experience of the breakfast buffet. They found that Zest was one of the most expensive all-you-can-eat Korean breakfast buffets in Seoul.

replay.tip: Try the mapa tofu and fresh noodles.

moments.title: La Seine Breakfast Buffet
moments.description: The creator visited La Seine to compare the food, quality, atmosphere, and overall experience of the breakfast buffet. They found that La Seine was another popular option for all-you-can-eat Korean breakfast buffets in Seoul.

replay.tip: Try the dim sum and pastries.

moments.title: The Parkview at The Shilla Breakfast Buffet
moments.description: The creator visited The Parkview at The Shilla to compare the food, quality, atmosphere, and overall experience of the breakfast buffet. They found that The Parkview at The Shilla was one of the most expensive all-you-can-eat Korean breakfast buffets in Seoul.

replay.tip: Try the Korean soups and seafood.

visits.spot: Korean UNDERGROUND FOOD

moments.title: Underground Food Hall
moments.description: The creator explored Seoul's underground food cities, discovering a wide variety of Korean street food and premium seafood. They found huge food halls packed with local favorites, including freshly baked pastries, Korean delicacies, and street food desserts.

replay.tip: Head straight to the basement of department stores for a culinary experience.

visits.spot: Texas Roadhouse in Korea

moments.title: Texas Roadhouse Experience
moments.description: The creator visited Texas Roadhouse in Pangyo, South Korea, and tried their pasta and steak. They were surprised by the quality of the food, especially the steak, which they found to be tender and juicy.

replay.tip: Try the cactus petals and Andouille shrimp pasta for a unique experience.

visits.spot: Korean Fried Chicken Tour

moments.title: KyoChon Chicken
moments.description: The creator visited KyoChon Chicken, a 24-hour Korean Fried Chicken restaurant, and tried their spicy and mala fried chicken. The creator enjoyed the crispy and juicy chicken, and appreciated the generous serving of potato wedges.

replay.tip: Try the mala flavor and pair it with a side of radish to cool down the spiciness.

moments.title: Yell - O Chicken
moments.description: The creator visited Yell - O Chicken in Itaewon and tried their sweet and spicy fried chicken. The creator enjoyed the saucy and juicy chicken, and appreciated the modern atmosphere of the restaurant.

replay.tip: Try the sweet and spicy flavor and enjoy the crispy outside and tender inside of the chicken.

visits.spot: Legendary Korean All You

moments.title: Lobster Buffet
moments.description: The creator visited Aria Buffet inside The Westin Josun Seoul and tried their all-you-can-eat lobster buffet. They enjoyed various dishes including tempura, seafood stir-fry, and a unique blueberry pizza.

replay.tip: Get the chili shrimp as soon as they bring it out and try the blueberry pizza.

visits.spot: All You Can Eat LOBSTER

moments.title: Lobster Buffet
moments.description: The creator visited The Parkview at The Shilla Seoul to try their all-you-can-eat lobster buffet. They were impressed by the variety and quality of the food, including the sushi, sashimi, and desserts.

place.name: The Parkview at The Shilla Seoul

replay.tip: Arrive early to avoid the crowd at the meat and lobster station.

moments.title: Sushi and Sashimi
moments.description: The creator enjoyed the sushi and sashimi at the buffet, praising the fish-to-rice ratio and the use of real wasabi. They also tried the assorted sushi and were impressed by the presentation and taste.

place.name: The Parkview at The Shilla Seoul

replay.tip: Try the assorted sushi and sashimi for a variety of flavors and textures.

moments.title: Desserts
moments.description: The creator was impressed by the dessert selection, which included gourmet desserts like matcha chocolate cake and peach tart. They also tried the hot chocolate souffle and were impressed by the quality of the chocolates used.

place.name: The Parkview at The Shilla Seoul

replay.tip: Save room for the desserts, which are made fresh in-house.

visits.spot: Foreign Couple Arrives

moments.title: Arrival at Incheon Airport
moments.description: The couple arrived at Incheon International Airport and were excited to start their journey in South Korea. They had to go through security and fill out an arrival card, which they noted was a strict process.

replay.tip: Make sure to fill out the arrival card properly to avoid being sent back to the queue.

moments.title: Buying SIM Card
moments.description: The couple purchased a SIM card at the KT SIM card shop, which included a free T-Money card. They found the process to be easy and were impressed with the fast internet speeds.

replay.tip: Consider purchasing a SIM card with a reputable provider like KT for easy and fast internet access.

moments.title: Traveling to Seoul
moments.description: The couple took the airport railroad to Seoul Station, which took around 60 minutes. They were impressed with the cleanliness and air-conditioning of the train.

replay.tip: Consider using the airport railroad for a convenient and affordable way to travel to Seoul.

moments.title: Trying Isaac's Toast
moments.description: The couple tried Isaac's Toast at Seoul Station, which they had heard was very popular. They were surrounded by many people and were excited to try the food.

replay.tip: Be prepared for a crowded environment when trying popular foods like Isaac's Toast.

young koreans are obsessed with this sleepy coastal town
soft summer days in gongju | a day in my life in korea

Busan locals' recommended dwaeji gukbap restaurant (부산 사람한테 추천받은 돼지국밥 맛집)
I FOUND HOME in KOREA'S COUNTRYSIDE | jeju island vacay vlog pt.2
I LEFT SEOUL...for BUSAN (a touristy vlog) | winter break @ KOREAN UNI
Inside Korea's Smallest Apartments
Busan old-restaurant owner surprised to see a foreigner (부산 노포 사장님)

moments.title: Walked into a 40-year-old pork soup spot
moments.description: Owner had never served a foreigner. Neighbourhood locals-only joint — no English menu, no tourist photos on the wall. Joel walked in, pointed at what the next table had, and got lucky.

place.name: 돼지국밥 district, Busan (search: 부산 돼지국밥 노포)

replay.tip: No English menu — show a phone photo of 돼지국밥. Order 순대 (blood sausage) as a side: locals always get both together. The broth refill is free, ask for it.

visits.spot: Korean 'jeong' found on a Bukhansan bike adventure + hidden valley (북한산 자전거 모험)
The South Korea you never knew existed | Sokcho (Seoraksan National Park) & Gyeongju in 2025
We stayed in Busan's WORST hotel | South Korea Travel Vlog (Busan & Jeju)
Everything you NEED to eat in Hongdae Seoul | street food night market guide

moments.title: Tteokbokki from a street cart
moments.description: The classic entry point. Spicy rice cakes in a flat iron pan, eaten standing up with chopsticks. The vendor had been running this cart for 20 years — same recipe, same spot.

place.name: Hongdae street food alley (걷고 싶은 거리)

replay.tip: Order the cheese-topped version if you can't handle heat — it cuts the spice and locals love it too

moments.title: Rooftop cafe overlooking the park
moments.description: Hongdae's cafe scene runs vertical — rooftop terraces one floor above the street give you the crowd energy below and enough quiet to actually talk. Open until 2am on weekends.

replay.tip: Go after 9pm — the streets light up and the park fills with buskers. The view from any third-floor cafe is worth it

moments.title: Buskers at the Hongdae main plaza
moments.description: Every weekend, the plaza in front of the park turns into an open-air stage. Dancers, singers, full bands — no ticket, no schedule. Just show up and a crowd will have already formed.

replay.tip: Friday and Saturday after 8pm. The hip-hop circle on the north side of the plaza is the most consistent — they've been going for years

visits.spot: We tried EVERY stall at Gwangjang Market (mayak gimbap, bindaetteok, raw beef) | Seoul food market

moments.title: Mayak gimbap — the addictive rolls
moments.description: Smaller than your thumb, sesame-heavy, rolled by hand every few minutes. 'Mayak' means narcotic in Korean — not an exaggeration. One order is ten pieces and you will order a second.

place.name: Gwangjang Market mayak gimbap row (광장시장 마약김밥)

replay.tip: The stalls on the left side as you enter Gate 1 have the shortest queue and the most experienced rollers. Dip in the soy-sesame sauce — don't skip it

moments.title: Bindaetteok — mung bean pancakes
moments.description: Thick, crispy, deeply savoury mung bean pancakes fried to order on a sizzling flat top. The crunch when you break one is audible from three stalls away. Eat immediately.

replay.tip: Ask for it with makgeolli (rice wine) — the vendors all have it and it's the traditional pairing. The bitterness cuts the oil perfectly

moments.title: Yukhoe — Korean steak tartare
moments.description: Raw beef, sliced thin, mixed with sesame oil, soy, garlic, pear, and a raw egg yolk. Served cold. It sounds extreme but the flavour is delicate — sweet, nutty, clean.

replay.tip: Only order from stalls that keep the beef in a chilled case — freshness is everything with raw meat. The vendors near the centre of the market are the most reliable

visits.spot: Busan's hidden hillside neighbourhood that looks like it was painted by hand | Gamcheon
The Naples of Korea nobody talks about — Tongyeong's seafood, islands, and cable car | iGoBart

moments.title: Chungmu gimbap at the harbour
moments.description: Tongyeong invented its own gimbap — thin, plain rice rolls served with spicy squid and radish kimchi on the side. Named after the old name for Tongyeong, it's only made properly here.

replay.tip: Buy from vendors near the fish market, not the tourist strip — the portions are bigger and the squid is cut that morning

moments.title: Hanryeo Waterway cable car
moments.description: The cable car crosses a strait between two hills, giving you a 360° view of Tongyeong harbour, the bridges, and the islands stretching south. On a clear day you can see Geoje Island.

replay.tip: Go in the late afternoon — the harbour light turns gold around 4pm. The glass-floor gondolas are worth paying extra for

moments.title: Grilled oysters at a pojangmacha
moments.description: Tongyeong produces most of Korea's oysters. The harbour has rows of pojangmacha (tent restaurants) grilling them on mesh over charcoal until they just pop open. Best eaten with soju and the sea breeze.

replay.tip: Look for a full tent — a crowd means the batch just came in from the boats. The vendor will give you a thin metal tool to pry them open

visits.spot: Korea's coffee capital on the east coast — Gangneung coffee street, Gyeongpo beach, and Chodang tofu | iGoBart
Trying EVERY street food in Myeongdong — lobster, tornado potato, egg bread | Korean Englishman

moments.title: Egg bread (계란빵) — the gateway snack
moments.description: A warm bread roll with a whole egg baked inside, sold from carts on every corner for ₩2,000. Fluffy, slightly sweet, the egg is just-set. It's the first thing Josh always buys when he arrives in Myeongdong.

replay.tip: The carts near the Myeongdong Cathedral end of the street have the longest queues for a reason — they bake in smaller batches so the bread is always fresh out

moments.title: Tornado potato on a stick
moments.description: A whole potato spiralled onto a skewer, deep fried, then coated in your choice of seasoning. Technically a snack, realistically a meal. The cheese-and-butter version is the unanimous choice.

replay.tip: The crispy spiral comes in savoury or sweet — go savoury (cheese + butter) on the first one. If the queue is more than 10 people, find another cart; they're on every block

moments.title: Skincare haul on the main drag
moments.description: Myeongdong's side streets are wall-to-wall K-beauty stores — Innisfree, Etude, Nature Republic, COSRX. Sheet masks are ₩1,000–2,000 each. Josh bought 40. No regrets.

replay.tip: Go to the smaller, less-branded stores for the same products at 30% less. The staff outside the big chains will push samples into your hands — take them, it's genuinely useful for comparing textures

visits.spot: Korea's most spectacular cherry blossom festival — Jinhae Gunhangje, the tunnel of flowers | Korean Englishman

moments.title: Yeojwacheon Stream — the blossom tunnel
moments.description: The stream runs under a canopy of cherry trees so dense the branches meet overhead. When it's peak bloom (late March to early April), petals fall like slow snow into the water. The photos don't capture the scale.

replay.tip: 6–7am before the crowds arrive — the stream is still, the light is soft, and you get the full tunnel to yourself for about 20 minutes. By 9am it's shoulder-to-shoulder

moments.title: Jinhae naval base road — petals on the asphalt
moments.description: The naval base opens parts of its grounds during the festival week. A kilometre-long road lined with cherry trees, pink and white petals covering the tarmac. Sailors in uniform walking through it adds to the surreal atmosphere.

place.name: Jinhae naval base cherry road (진해 군항제)

replay.tip: Only open during festival week (usually last week of March / first week of April — check the year's date in advance). Arrive at the gate before 8am to walk before the crowds

visits.spot: The ULTIMATE Korean street food market — everything I ate at Gwangjang Market Seoul | Mark Wiens

moments.title: Sundae — Korean blood sausage
moments.description: Pork intestine stuffed with glass noodles, barley, and blood. Steamed, sliced thick, eaten with a pinch of salt and ground perilla. It's earthy, slightly mineral, nothing like Western blood sausage.

replay.tip: Ask for 모둠 (modeum — mixed plate) which comes with sundae, liver, and lung. The salt-and-perilla dip is the correct way to eat it — skip the ssamjang, it overpowers the flavour

moments.title: Nokdu bindaetteok — the mung bean pancake deep cut
moments.description: Mark's face when he took the first bite of a fresh-off-the-griddle bindaetteok: a full second of silence, then 'this is one of the best things I've ever eaten.' Crispy outside, custardy inside, savoury throughout.

replay.tip: The pancake should sizzle when it hits your mouth — if it doesn't, it sat too long. Watch the griddle and grab yours the moment it comes off. Eat standing up, eat fast

moments.title: Silkworm pupae (번데기) — the street snack divider
moments.description: A paper cup of steamed silkworm pupae, eaten warm from the cart. Nutty, earthy, intensely savoury. Mark ate an entire cup. Half the comments on the video are 'I would never' and the other half are 'where do I get these.'

replay.tip: They smell stronger than they taste. The flavour is closer to roasted nuts than anything else — try one before deciding. Cart vendors near the east gate have the freshest batches

visits.spot: Seoul's legendary fish market — buying live seafood and eating it right there | Mark Wiens
